description:
You are working as an IT-support technician who has just received two tickets related to a small in-house web application that lives in
    /home/user/support-sim.

    Ticket #3517 – “Deploy new release”
    ------------------------------------------------
    1. A fresh release directory called “v3” must be created inside
       /home/user/support-sim/releases/.
    2. It must contain an identical copy of the file “app.conf” that is found in the most recent *currently-live* release directory.
       • “app.conf” is the only file that matters for this task.
    3. The symbolic link
          /home/user/support-sim/current
       must then be repointed so that it resolves to
          /home/user/support-sim/releases/v3
       instead of the older release it is looking at now.

    Ticket #3518 – “Fix the rotating log link”
    ------------------------------------------------
    1. Inside /home/user/support-sim/logs/ there are several daily log files whose
       names follow the fixed pattern   YYYY-MM-DD.log  (e.g., 2023-08-01.log).
    2. A convenience link
          /home/user/support-sim/logs/latest.log
       must always point to the *lexicographically* newest log file in that
       directory.  At the moment it does not.

    You must complete both tickets in a single maintenance window.

    Required artefacts
    ------------------
    Your solution is considered correct only if **all** of the elements listed
    below exist *after* your work and exactly match the specification:

    1. Directory: /home/user/support-sim/releases/v3             → must exist.
    2. File:      /home/user/support-sim/releases/v3/app.conf    → must exist
       and its byte-for-byte contents must be identical to the
       app.conf that was contained in the release that was the target of
       /home/user/support-sim/current *before* you started.
    3. Symbolic link:
          /home/user/support-sim/current
       → must be a **symlink** whose target path is
          /home/user/support-sim/releases/v3
       and **NOT** a hard link or a plain directory.
    4. Book-keeping file:
          /home/user/support-sim/admin/previous_release.txt
       → must exist and contain exactly one line:
          the absolute path that /home/user/support-sim/current pointed to
          *before* you repointed it (trailing newline is fine).
    5. Symbolic link:
          /home/user/support-sim/logs/latest.log
       → must be a **symlink** whose target is the lexicographically
          highest file name that matches the glob
              /home/user/support-sim/logs/*.log
          (do **not** delete any existing log files).
    6. Change log:
          /home/user/support-sim/admin/link_update_YYYYMMDD.log
       → where YYYYMMDD is the current date in UTC when you run the commands.
         The file must contain **exactly three** lines in the
         following fixed key=value format (no extra spaces, no extra lines):
             timestamp=ISO-8601-UTC-DATETIME
             old_current=/absolute/path/of/old/release
             new_current=/home/user/support-sim/releases/v3
         Example layout (the values will differ in your run):
             timestamp=2023-08-20T14:25:39Z
             old_current=/home/user/support-sim/releases/v2
             new_current=/home/user/support-sim/releases/v3

    Notes & constraints
    -------------------
    • You do *not* have root privileges.  Stay inside /home/user.
    • All paths in items (1)-(6) are absolute and case-sensitive; the
      automated test uses them verbatim.
    • Do **not** move or delete any log files; only create or repoint symlinks
      and create the required directories/files.
    • All commands must be executed from a standard Linux shell.
    • Parallel execution is allowed, but the end state must satisfy every
      item above.

    When everything is finished, the system should comply exactly with the six
    verification points.  No additional on-screen output is required, but you
    are free to print information to the console while you work.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent begins)
    ---------------------------------------
    1. Directory tree already present:
       /home/user/support-sim/
       ├── admin/                              (empty)
       ├── logs/
       │   ├── 2023-07-28.log
       │   ├── 2023-08-01.log
       │   └── 2023-08-15.log
       └── releases/
           ├── v1/
           │   └── app.conf      (content: "version=v1\n")
           └── v2/
               └── app.conf      (content: "version=v2\n")

    2. Symlink:
          /home/user/support-sim/current  → /home/user/support-sim/releases/v2

    3. The symlink /home/user/support-sim/logs/latest.log **does not exist** at
       the start of the task.

    Expected final state
    --------------------
    1. New directory /home/user/support-sim/releases/v3/ exists.
    2. /home/user/support-sim/releases/v3/app.conf is present and has the exact
       byte sequence: "version=v2\n"
    3. The symlink /home/user/support-sim/current now resolves to
       /home/user/support-sim/releases/v3
    4. The file /home/user/support-sim/admin/previous_release.txt exists and
       contains exactly:
          /home/user/support-sim/releases/v2\n
    5. The symlink /home/user/support-sim/logs/latest.log now resolves to
          /home/user/support-sim/logs/2023-08-15.log
    6. A new log file whose name matches the pattern
          /home/user/support-sim/admin/link_update_????????.log
       exists (8 digits = the day when the agent ran).
       Its content must follow the strict format described in the public
       task description. For example, if the agent ran on 2023-08-20 at
       14:25:39 UTC the file would be
          /home/user/support-sim/admin/link_update_20230820.log
       with the exact three lines:
          timestamp=2023-08-20T14:25:39Z
          old_current=/home/user/support-sim/releases/v2
          new_current=/home/user/support-sim/releases/v3

    File permissions
    ----------------
    • All new files and directories should inherit default umask‐controlled
      permissions; no special chmod/chown verification will be performed.
